content: To whom did the angel announce the birth of Jesus?
explanation: Luke 1:26-38 — The angel Gabriel announced the news to Mary.

content: Who first received the angels' good news of the Nativity?
explanation: Luke 2:8-14 — The shepherds heard the angel announce the good news.

content: When Jesus was baptized, the Holy Spirit descended in what form?
explanation: Luke 3:22 — The Holy Spirit descended in bodily form like a dove.

content: What miracle made Peter fall to his knees, saying 'Depart from me, Lord'?
explanation: Luke 5:6-8 — The miraculous catch of fish made Peter tremble before holiness.

content: What did Jesus teach about enemies?
explanation: Luke 6:27 — 'Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you.'

content: What is the model prayer Jesus taught called?
explanation: Luke 11:2-4 — The prayer: 'Our Father in heaven...'

content: What did the sinful woman do to Jesus' feet at Simon's house?
explanation: Luke 7:38 — Wept on them, wiped with her hair, kissed, and anointed with perfume.
options: Wet them with tears, wiped with her hair, anointed with oil

content: In the parable of the sower, what happened to the seeds that fell on the path?
explanation: Luke 8:5,12 — Birds devoured them; the evil one snatches the word from the heart.

content: Who appeared at the Transfiguration in Luke?
explanation: Luke 9:30 — The two men were Moses and Elijah.

content: In the parable of the Good Samaritan, who helped the man in need?
explanation: Luke 10:33 — The Samaritan saw and had compassion.

content: How did Jesus teach about anxiety over food and clothing?
explanation: Luke 12:29-31 — Seek first the kingdom of God.
options: Do not worry; first seek the kingdom of God

content: In the lesson about the seat at the feast, what does the Lord teach?
explanation: Luke 14:10 — Take the lowest place, so the host may say 'Friend, move up higher.'

content: In the parable of the prodigal son, what does the father do when the son returns?
explanation: Luke 15:20-24 — The father had compassion, ran out, embraced and kissed him, threw a feast.
options: Ran out, embraced and kissed him, threw a feast

content: In the story of the rich man and Lazarus, where were they after death?
explanation: Luke 16:22-23 — The rich man was in torment; Lazarus was at Abraham's side.
options: The rich man in torment, Lazarus at Abraham's side

content: What does the parable of the unjust judge teach about prayer?
explanation: Luke 18:1 — He told them they ought always to pray and not lose heart.

content: Who was Zacchaeus and what did he do after meeting Jesus?
explanation: Luke 19:2,8 — Zacchaeus promised to repay fourfold and give to the poor.
options: A merchant who built a synagogue
Chief tax collector who restored fourfold and gave to the poor

content: How did Jesus answer about paying taxes to Caesar?
explanation: Luke 20:25 — A double principle of civic and divine responsibility.
options: Give it all to the temple
Render to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's

content: At Gethsemane, what did Jesus teach His disciples?
explanation: Luke 22:40 — Pray that you may not enter into temptation.
options: Pray that you may not enter into temptation

content: What did Jesus say to the repentant thief on the cross?
explanation: Luke 23:43 — A promise of paradise to the repentant.
options: Today you will be with Me in Paradise

content: When did the two disciples on the Emmaus road recognize Jesus?
explanation: Luke 24:30-31 — Their eyes were opened when He broke the bread.

content: In Luke 2:14, 'Glory to God in the highest.' What does the Greek word for 'glory' mean?
explanation: Doxa (δόξα) means 'glory, honor' — the splendor and majesty of God.

content: In Luke 15:20, 'The father ran and embraced him.' What does the Greek for 'ran' mean?
explanation: Dramo (δραμών) means 'running quickly,' showing the father's haste and love.

content: Where was Jesus born?
explanation: Luke 2:7 — Mary gave birth to her firstborn son, wrapped Him in cloths, and laid Him in a manger because there was no room in the inn.

content: In the parable of the Good Samaritan, who helped the man who had been robbed?
explanation: Luke 10:33-34 — The Samaritan (despised by the Jews) was the only one who showed compassion and helped.

content: In the parable of the lost sheep, how many sheep did the shepherd have before losing one?
explanation: Luke 15:4 — The shepherd had 100, lost 1, left the 99 to go after the lost one. Illustrates God's love for the sinner.

content: In the parable of the prodigal son, when the son returned, what did the father do?
explanation: Luke 15:20-24 — The father ran out and embraced him, put on the best robe, the ring, and held a feast. A beautiful image of God welcoming the repentant sinner.
options: Ran out, embraced him, and held a feast

content: What did Jesus promise the repentant thief on the cross?
explanation: Luke 23:43 — Salvation requires only sincere faith, not works. The thief simply believed and was promised paradise.
options: Today you will be with Me in Paradise

content: Jesus read Isaiah 61 at Nazareth and declared He came to do what?
explanation: Luke 4:18-19 — Jesus declared His mission using Isaiah's words: total liberation and healing.
options: Preach the gospel to the poor, heal the brokenhearted, free captives

content: Luke 6:38: '___ and it will be given to you; good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will be put into your lap'
explanation: The principle of generosity: give and you will receive abundantly. God blesses generous hearts.

content: Between Martha distracted with serving and Mary sitting and listening, whom did Jesus commend?
explanation: Luke 10:42 — Mary chose the better part: sitting at the Lord's feet to hear His Word. Relationship is greater than service.
options: Mary, for choosing the better part

content: Jesus warned: 'Take care, and be on your guard against all ___'
explanation: Luke 12:15 — True life does not consist in possessions. The lesson of the parable of the rich fool.
options: Covetousness, for one's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions

content: Of the ten lepers healed, how many returned to give thanks?
explanation: Luke 17:15-16 — Only one of ten was thankful. Jesus asked: where are the other nine? A lesson on gratitude.
options: Only one (and he was a Samaritan)

content: In the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector, who was justified?
explanation: Luke 18:14 — The tax collector simply cried, 'God, be merciful to me, a sinner.' And was justified.
options: The Pharisee, for doing many good works
The tax collector, for humble repentance
